seo_title: MEMO Framework for Training Dedicated Memory Models
meta_description: A modular framework for training dedicated memory models on new knowledge without modifying LLM parameters.
body_html:
The researchers propose a modular framework called MEMO that encodes corpus knowledge into a separate trainable MEMORY model. This allows for training dedicated memory models on new knowledge without modifying LLM parameters.

Key Benefits of the MEMO Framework

  • Separates concerns between corpus knowledge and new knowledge, enabling more efficient training
  • Promotes modularity and reusability in memory model development
  • Enables training on new knowledge without modifying LLM parameters

Comparison to Existing Frameworks

FrameworkDescription
MonoA free and open-source software framework that aims to run software made for the .NET Framework on Linux and other OSes.
OpenAlexA conceptual framework for enterprise modeling that provides abstractions for designing corporate information systems.

Limitations of Existing Methods

Large language models transfer well to new tasks out-of-the-box, but prompting is a brittle process that requires significant effort towards designing effective prompts.

The proposed MEMO framework addresses this limitation by developing an understanding of effective prompt formats and proposing a simple strategy for prompting language models called ASK ME ANYTHING (AMA).

Our observations motivate our proposed prompting method, which can lead to high-quality prompting strategies with minimal effort.

By aggregating multiple effective prompts, we can mitigate the high degree of effort involved in prompt-design and promote more efficient training of dedicated memory models.

source.title: MEMO: A Modular Framework for Training a Dedicated Memory Model on New Knowledge Without Modifying LLM Parameters
source.summary: Researchers from NUS, MIT, and A*STAR have proposed MEMO, a modular framework that allows for training a dedicated memory model on new knowledge without modifying large language model (LLM) parameters. This framework encodes corpus knowledge into a separate trainable MEMORY model. The goal of MEMO is to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of LLMs in handling new information.
